project: Influx v1 — Error Hierarchy, CLI Dispatcher, Guarded HTTP Client
description: Extend the InfluxError hierarchy with NetworkError, LithosError, LCMAError, and ExtractionError; replace the PRD 01 minimal dispatcher with the full v1 CLI (serve, run, backfill, validate-config, migrate-notes) using stubs for subcommands that later PRDs fill in; and ship a guarded HTTP client in src/influx/http_client.py that enforces scheme allow-list, SSRF IP-classification (with security.allow_private_ips override), streaming size cap, connect + read timeout, content-type family check (HTML/PDF/XML/Atom), and redirect re-validation at every hop.

userStories.title: Extend errors.py with NetworkError, LithosError, LCMAError, ExtractionError
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the InfluxError hierarchy extended with NetworkError, LithosError, LCMAError, and ExtractionError subclasses so that retry/abort policy in the scheduler and HTTP API can discriminate failure categories and log structured context fields.
acceptanceCriteria: src/influx/errors.py defines NetworkError, LithosError, LCMAError, and ExtractionError, each a subclass of InfluxError (isinstance chain to InfluxError holds for every new subclass)
All four new subclasses are importable from influx.errors
NetworkError carries enough context to log the offending URL and the violated constraint (e.g. a kind/reason field plus url; exact attribute names left to the implementation)

LithosError, LCMAError, and ExtractionError each carry enough context to log structured fields per PRD §5.1 (the source PRD only gives a concrete example for NetworkError; the exact field names for these three subclasses are left to the implementation and may be deferred until the PRDs that introduce their log sites)

tests/unit/test_errors.py covers: isinstance chain to InfluxError for each new subclass; each subclass exposes the structured context its log sites need (attribute access or equivalent; the exact constructor shape is left to the implementation)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add httpx dependency and create guarded HTTP client with SSRF guard
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an httpx-backed guarded HTTP client in src/influx/http_client.py that enforces the http/https scheme allow-list and resolves hostnames to IPs before issuing the request, rejecting loopback, link-local, private, and multicast addresses unless security.allow_private_ips is true, so that every outbound fetch has one SSRF choke point.
acceptanceCriteria: httpx is added to project dependencies in pyproject.toml and resolves on install
src/influx/http_client.py exposes a guarded fetch API that returns an object with body, status_code, content_type, and final_url attributes (per PRD §5.4)
The guarded client rejects requests whose scheme is not http or https (exact exception type and rejection timing are left to the implementation unless the source PRD specifies them)

Hostnames are resolved via socket.getaddrinfo BEFORE issuing the request and IPs are classified using the ipaddress module's is_loopback, is_link_local, is_private, and is_multicast methods (per PRD §5.4)

When security.allow_private_ips is false, requests to loopback, link-local, private, or multicast IPs raise NetworkError with enough structured context for logging; for the concrete AC-X-3 examples (http://169.254.169.254/... and http://127.0.0.1/...) that context MUST include the offending URL and the resolved IP

When security.allow_private_ips is true, the SSRF guard's rejection is bypassed for loopback, link-local, private, and multicast addresses; in particular, a request to http://127.0.0.1/... succeeds (AC-02-A)

tests/unit/test_http_client.py covers SSRF rejection for each of the four IP classes (loopback, link-local, private, multicast) when allow_private_ips=false, and the allow-through path when allow_private_ips=true
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add streaming size cap and connect + read timeout to guarded HTTP client
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the guarded HTTP client to stream response bodies and abort mid-stream when storage.max_download_bytes is exceeded, and to enforce storage.download_timeout_seconds as both connect and read timeout, so that oversize or slow responses cannot stall or exhaust the process.
acceptanceCriteria: The guarded client streams response bodies rather than buffering the full response (per PRD §5.4)
A response whose cumulative body exceeds storage.max_download_bytes is aborted mid-stream and raises NetworkError with kind 'oversize' (AC-02-B); the partial body is NOT returned to the caller
Connect timeout and read timeout are both bounded by storage.download_timeout_seconds (FR-RES-4); timeout raises NetworkError
tests/unit/test_http_client.py covers size-cap streaming abort (using a fixture server, respx, or equivalent) and timeout behaviour; oversize case asserts that no body is returned
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Enforce content-type family check (HTML, PDF, XML/Atom)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the guarded HTTP client to accept an expected content-type family argument from the caller and raise NetworkError when the response's content-type does not belong to that family, so that callers expecting HTML never silently receive a PDF (or vice versa).
acceptanceCriteria: The guarded fetch API accepts an expected content-type family argument with at least HTML, PDF, and XML/Atom families (exact enum/literal naming left to the implementation)

The check is family-based: a response's content-type is matched against the caller-specified family (HTML, PDF, or XML/Atom); the exact MIME mapping per family is left to the implementation and is not constrained by this PRD
A response whose content-type does not belong to the expected family raises NetworkError with kind 'content_type_mismatch' (AC-02-D)
tests/unit/test_http_client.py covers positive cases for each family (HTML/PDF/XML) and at least one mismatch case (e.g. expected HTML, received application/pdf)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Re-validate scheme and IP at every redirect hop
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the guarded HTTP client to re-apply the scheme allow-list and the SSRF IP classification on every redirect target, so that a redirect from a public host to a private IP (R-4) fails at the redirect hop just as a direct connection to that IP would.
acceptanceCriteria: Every redirect target has its scheme re-validated against the http/https allow-list and its hostname re-resolved and re-classified against the SSRF IP rules (per PRD §5.3 R-4 mitigation)
A redirect from a public host to a loopback or private IP raises NetworkError at the redirect hop (AC-02-C), not just on the initial request
security.allow_private_ips=true is honoured consistently at every hop (same semantics as for the initial request)
tests/unit/test_http_client.py covers a redirect from a public host to a private IP and asserts the failure is attributable to the redirect hop (e.g. the NetworkError carries the redirect target URL)
Coverage on src/influx/http_client.py is >= 80%
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Extend CLI argparse surface with serve, run, backfill subcommand stubs
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the final v1 CLI contract (serve, run --profile, backfill --profile --days/--from/--to [--confirm]) wired into the argparse dispatcher now — with stub handler bodies that print a clear message and exit 64 — so that no later PRD changes user-visible flags when it fills the bodies in.
acceptanceCriteria: src/influx/main.py (or a new src/influx/cli.py if the dispatcher is extracted; main.py may become a thin entry per PRD §6) registers three new subcommands: serve, run (with --profile), and backfill (with --profile, --days, --from, --to, --confirm)

The argparse surface for serve, run, and backfill matches the final v1 CLI contract per PRD §4 and the Definition of Done: user-visible flag names, help text, and any argument behaviour already specified in FR-CLI-2/3/4 (serve takes no flags; run takes --profile; backfill takes --profile, --days, --from, --to, --confirm) are locked in by this PRD so that no later PRD changes the user-visible CLI surface; only the handler bodies may remain stubs

The backfill subcommand registers the --confirm flag as part of its final argparse surface so that later PRDs do not change the user-visible CLI contract; the FR-CLI-4 large-job --confirm enforcement semantics (when --confirm is required, any 'large job' threshold, and the associated rejection/acceptance tests) are deferred to the later PRD that wires POST /backfills
Handler bodies for run and backfill are stubs that print a clear stub / not-implemented message and exit with code 64 (per PRD §2 / §5.2)

The serve subcommand is registered with its final argparse surface (takes no flags per FR-CLI-2) and routes to a stub handler in this PRD; the exact stub body behaviour is left to the implementation because the source PRD is internally inconsistent on this point (see story note), and PRD 03 replaces the handler body regardless
The pre-existing validate-config subcommand continues to route to the existing validator unchanged
tests/unit/test_cli_dispatch.py covers: serve, run, and backfill each route to their registered handler (argparse does not reject the flags); the run and backfill stub handlers exit 64
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Source PRD is internally inconsistent about the serve stub body: §2 describes printing a 'starting' message and blocking on a signal, while FR-CLI-2 specifies printing '[stub] serve not wired yet' and exiting 64. The exact stub behaviour needs clarification in the source PRD; the argparse surface is locked in here, and PRD 03 replaces the handler body regardless.

userStories.title: Add migrate-notes subcommand, exit-code policy, and unknown-subcommand handling
userStories.description: As an operator, I want `python -m influx migrate-notes` to print `note_schema_version: <N>` (using the value from config, default 1) and exit 0, and I want the dispatcher to return exit code 64 for unknown subcommands while preserving AC-X-5 (no subcommand → help + non-zero), so that the CLI contract in FR-CLI-6 and FR-CLI-7 is fully satisfied.
acceptanceCriteria: The dispatcher registers a migrate-notes subcommand whose handler prints exactly 'note_schema_version: <N>' (where N is influx.note_schema_version from the loaded config, default 1) and exits 0 (AC-02-F, FR-CLI-6)
Exit-code policy is enforced per FR-CLI-7: 0 success, 1 partial failure, 2 total failure, 64 usage error (exit codes may be defined as named constants in the dispatcher module)
`python -m influx <unknown>` exits 64 (AC-02-E)
`python -m influx` with no subcommand continues to print help and exit non-zero (AC-X-5 regression)
tests/unit/test_cli_dispatch.py covers AC-02-F (migrate-notes output and exit 0), AC-02-E (unknown subcommand → exit 64), and the AC-X-5 no-args regression
Coverage on the CLI dispatcher module (src/influx/main.py and/or src/influx/cli.py) is >= 80%
Typecheck passes
